channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Carolina scheduling executions again after a pause for the holidays
item.description: South Carolina is beginning to schedule executions again after a pause for the holidays, with Marion Bowman Jr. set to be put to death on Jan. 31.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Golden Globes are Sunday night. Here are five things to look for and how to watch them
item.description: After a rocky few years and the disbanding of the Hollywood Foreign Press Association, the Golden Globes have seemingly stabilized.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: British man and a nursing assistant were the last victims identified in New Orleans attack
item.description: A British man who was killed when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street packed with holiday revelers was identified Saturday as the stepson of a former nanny to the Royal Family. Prince William expressed shock and sadness at the news of the death of Edward Pettifer of London.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral begins by tracing 100 years from rural Georgia to the world stage
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter 's extended public farewell began Saturday in Georgia, with the 39th U.S. president’s flag-draped casket tracing his long arc from the Depression-era South and family farming business to the pinnacle of American political power and decades as a global humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JEFF AMY and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Washington Post cartoonist quits after paper rejects sketch of Bezos bowing to Trump
item.description: A cartoonist has decided to quit her job at the Washington Post after an editor rejected her sketch of the newspaper's owner and other media executives bowing before President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More than 500 animals killed during fire at Texas shopping center
item.description: More than 500 animals at a Dallas shopping center pet shop were killed when a fire broke out at the structure, fire authorities said this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drivers skid and crash as wintry mix grips central US before moving east
item.description: Road conditions are beginning to deteriorate in the central U.S. as a mixture of snow, ice and plunging temperatures arrived.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth And Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington will receive the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington will be awarded the nation's highest civilian honor on Saturday in a White House ceremony.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: New Orleans attacker’s apparent loyalty to Islamic State group highlights persistent threat of lone wolf terrorism
item.description: The deadly Jan. 1, 2025, attack in New Orleans serves as a reminder of the persistent threat to the U.S. from individuals inspired by extremist Islamist groups.
item.dc:creator: Sara Harmouch, American University, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans mourns victims of truck attack with tearful vigil and celebration of life
item.description: New Orleans has held a vigil on Bourbon Street to mourn and celebrate the lives of those killed when a man rammed a pickup truck into a crowd, killing and injuring revelers celebrating the new year.
item.dc:creator: Jim Mustian, Jack Brook, Stephen Smith And Sara Cline, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: IRS reminds taxpayers in 24 disaster area states of 2025 deadlines
item.description: A tax filing extension for residents of two dozen states affected by disasters is coming to an end in 2025, the IRS reminded taxpayers Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Soldier who blew up Tesla at Trump hotel left note saying blast was to be a 'wake up call' for US
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A highly decorated Army soldier who fatally shot himself in a Tesla Cybertruck just before it blew up outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas left notes saying the New Year's Day explosion was a stunt to serve as a “wake up call” for the country’s ills, investigators said Friday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, TARA COPP, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: University of Michigan lab develops blood test to identify HPV-related cancers
item.description: Researchers at a University of Michigan lab have developed a “first-of-its-kind” blood test to detect head and neck cancers caused by human papillomavirus.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Nearly 700 Party City store leases heading to auction
item.description: Nearly 700 Party City store leases will be heading to auction next month, according to an announcement Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, WGN 9

item.title: Finalized tax credit for cleaner hydrogen gets a cautious OK from some environmental groups
item.description: The Biden administration released long-awaited final rules Friday for a tax credit that will send billions of dollars to producers of cleaner hydrogen.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Verizon to bring Super Bowl FanFest to Sofi, other NFL stadiums nationwide
item.description: The Caesars Superdome may be the home for the Super Bowl in New Orleans next month, but this year, festivities will extend far beyond the Bayou State, thanks to Verizon.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: It's official: 2024's holiday travel season was the busiest ever recorded
item.description: Those who were at the airport in recent weeks felt it, and the numbers back it up: 2024's holiday travel season was the busiest on record. That information came on Friday by way of the TSA, which made the claim on social media.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Jill Biden got most expensive gift from a foreign leader in 2023. Here's what it was
item.description: President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamond from India’s le
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: FBI checks, ethics paperwork threaten to slow down Trump confirmations
item.description: Republican sources on Capitol Hill warn that President-elect Trump’s nominees may be delayed because of paperwork holdups and the slow start of FBI background checks, creating obstacles for the speedy confirmation of Trump’s national security team.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Biden awards Medal of Honor to seven US Army soldiers
item.description: President Biden on Friday awarded the Medal of Honor to seven U.S. Army soldiers, most of whom received it posthumously for service during the Korea War, in his last ceremony as commander-in-chief awarding the country’s highest military honor.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: FBI releases new video of suspect planting pipe bomb on eve of Capitol riot
item.description: A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Surgeon general calls for cancer warnings on alcohol
item.description: Alcoholic beverages should carry a prominent warning label about the link between alcohol and cancer, according to the U.S. surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Here’s what Congress’s first day will look like
item.description: The 119th Congress kicks off Friday as lawmakers return to Washington with a long January to-do list and a packed first day. Confirmation hearings for President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees are set to get rolling in the coming weeks, and Trump himself will be inaugurated in less than three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: A data company has figured out which airlines fly on time most often
item.description: Aeromexico is the most on-time airline in the world, according to a data company that compiled figures for 2024. Cirium said in its annual ranking released on Thursday that nearly 87% of the Mexican airline's flights were on time in 2024.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to adjust your budget if you're unemployed
item.description: More than a quarter (26%) of households live "paycheck to paycheck," spending over 95% of their income on necessities.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about the New Year's Day attack in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Investigators now believe the driver who barreled through a crowd of New Year’s Day revelers in New Orleans acted alone and found he posted several videos on social media pledging his support for the Islamic State group, the FBI said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sugar Bowl security to equal Super Bowl: New Orleans police
item.description: The New Orleans Police Department revealed Thursday that the annual Sugar Bowl will feature heightened security after a man drove directly into a crowd on Bourbon Street earlier this week, leaving 15 dead.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Soldier in Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion had gunshot wound to head: sheriff
item.description: The man who drove a Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside of a Las Vegas, Nevada hotel has been identified as Matthew Livelsberger, an Army soldier from Colorado, a law enforcement source told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans attack victims: What we know so far
item.description: Investigations continue into an "act of terrorism" in New Orleans' bustling Canal and Bourbon streets after a truck drove into a crowd, killing 15 people and injuring at least 35 more.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Duchess Meghan returns to Instagram
item.description: (The Hill) -- Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, announced her return to Instagram on New Year’s Day with a celebratory post on the beach. The former actress Meghan Markle jogged across the sand in an all-white outfit with her hair flowing in the wind before writing 2025 in the sand.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Meet the newest breed in American Kennel Club's lineup
item.description: Say hello to the latest dog in the American Kennel Club's lineup of recognized breeds. Or you might say “hej.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Meet the Danish-Swedish farmdog, the newest breed in the American Kennel Club's lineup
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Say hello to the latest dog in the American Kennel Club's lineup of recognized breeds. Or you might say “hej.” The Danish-Swedish farmdog — yep, that's the official name — joined the pack Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 10 people shot outside New York City nightclub on New Year's Day: NYPD
item.description: At least 10 people were shot outside a Queens nightclub on New Year's Day, according to the New York City Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Multiple dead, injured after car plowed into crowd on Bourbon Street in act of terrorism
item.description: Multiple people are dead after the New Orleans Police Department reports a car may have plowed into a group of people on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, WGN 9

item.title: A drop in groundwater could mean big trouble for a tiny snail as Nevada lithium project presses on
item.description: RENO, Nev. (AP) — Opponents of the nation's largest lithium mine under construction want U.S. officials to investigate whether the project in Nevada already has caused a drop in groundwater levels that could lead to extinction of a tiny snail being considered for endangered species protection.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT SONNER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Orleans barriers to prevent vehicle attacks were in the process of being replaced
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Seven years ago, New Orleans officials began installing adjustable barriers at intersections in the famed French Quarter to temporarily prevent vehicles from entering the tourist area where the narrow streets are typically teeming with pedestrians every night.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JEFF MARTIN and KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, ABC 4
